message:
feat(tui): schema-shielding layer for dashboard data models (bd-xzt.2.9)

DaemonState and sub-structs now use #[serde(default)] so that missing or
added fields in state.json don't hard-fail deserialization. The adapter
layer detects schema drift (unknown/missing top-level keys) and surfaces
SchemaWarnings in DashboardSnapshot for operator visibility.

Changes:
- self_monitor.rs: Add Default derives and #[serde(default)] to all
  state file structs (DaemonState, PressureState, BallastState,
  LastScanState, Counters)
- adapters.rs: Add SchemaWarnings type with has_drift() helper,
  detect_schema_drift() function comparing JSON keys to expected fields,
  and warnings field on DashboardSnapshot
- adapters.rs: 9 new tests covering extra fields, missing fields,
  minimal JSON, combined drift, stale+drift, and edge cases
- telemetry.rs: JSONL schema-shield parser with lenient field recovery
  (concurrent agent work)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
